rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM WEST JR. et al VS. CONTEMPORARY SERVICES CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Conditional Certification Of Class For Settlement Purposes; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHNSON, ANGELA, AS INDIVIDUAL AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Conditional Certification Of Class For Settlement Purposes. Plaintiffs' motion for preliminary approval of class action settlement and conditional certification of class for settlement purposes is denied without prejudice to a further evidentiary showing. In addition, a hearing is required to address a number of issues raised by the motion. In order to determine whether a proposed class action settlement is fair and reasonable, a trial court "should consider relevant factors, such as the strength of plaintiffs' case, the risk, expense, complexity and likely duration of further litigation, the risk of maintaining class action status through trial, the amount offered in settlement, the extent of discovery completed and the stage of the proceedings, the experience and views of counsel, the presence of a governmental participant, and the reaction of the class members to the proposed settlement." (Dunk v. Ford Motor Co. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 1794, 1801.) "'The most important factor is the strength of the case for plaintiffs on the merits, balanced against the amount offered in settlement.'" (Kullar v. Foot Locker Retail, Inc. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 116, 130.) While a presumption of fairness exists if certain factors are present, "an informed evaluation cannot be made without an understanding of the amount that is in controversy and the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation." (Id. at 120.) The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD, ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants Talesun Solar USA Ltd. and Zhongli New Energy USA Co., LLC's motion for summary adjudication on Plaintiff's first cause of action for breach of the Master Membership Interest and Partnership Agreement ("MIPA") is denied. Defendants fail to maintain their initial summary judgment burden. Defendants do not show that they breached the MIPA on November 30, 2012 and Plaintiff filed this action in an untimely fashion more than four years later. The parties originally contemplated that the first part of the MIPA would close "on or around" November 30, 2012. The record demonstrates that the parties subsequently agreed to continue the closing date numerous times. (See Plaintiff's Ex. K [e-mail string re initial closing date]; Plaintiff's Ex. L, Chen Depo., 215:11-216:8 [Talesun employee admits that the parties agreed that the $46,160 could be paid later]; Plaintiff's Ex. M [second Recital D, §1.1(d), and exhibit B of the Standstill Agreement demonstrates that the $46,160 could be paid later]; Plaintiff's Ex. O [exhibit C to the Letter Agreement demonstrates that the $46,160 could be paid later].) In addition, the original version of the MIPA contemplated payments on December 27, 2012 and December 31, 2012. The MIPA is fairly characterized as an installment contract and it is undisputed that a claim based on the December 27 and 31 payments is timely. Talesun cannot summarily adjudicate the entire first cause of action even if it did breach one part of the contract. (See Romano v. Rockwell Internat., Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 479, 489 [where the parties have ongoing contractual obligations, the plaintiff need not sue after an initial breach and it may elect to rely on the contract and sue at the time of complete performance]; Armstrong Petroleum Corp. v. Tri-Valley Oil & Gas Co. (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 1375, 1388 ["[W]here performance of contractual obligations is severed into intervals, as in installment contracts, the courts have found that an action attacking the performance for any particular interval must be brought within the period of limitations after the particular performance was due."]; White v. Moriarty (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 1290, 1299 ["Where money is payable in installments, the statute of limitations begins to run against the cause of action for the recovery of an unpaid installment at the time it is payable."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Its Second Cause Of Action Against Talesun Solar Usa Ltd
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Its Second Cause Of Action Against Talesun Solar USA Ltd. Plaintiff Martifer-Silverado Fund I, LLC's motion for summary adjudication on its second cause of action for breach of the Standstill Agreement is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the amount of Plaintiff's damages. Plaintiff seeks $11,471,416.50 on this claim. That sum is derived from the Standstill Agreement and includes the base purchase price for the MIPA projects and $500,000 for Talesun's prior nonperformance. (See Standstill Agreement, Ex. B and § 1.1(d).) An injured party may recover for a breach of contract the amount which will compensate it "for all the detriment proximately caused [by the breach], or which, in the ordinary course of things, would be likely to result [from the breach]." (Civ. Code § 3300.) The damages awarded should, insofar as possible, place the injured party in the same position it would have held had the contract properly been performed, but such damage may not exceed the benefit which it would have received had the promisor performed. (Civ. Code § 3358.) "The right to recover damages in civil actions is qualified by the common law doctrine of avoidable consequences. Under the doctrine, 'a person injured by another's wrongful conduct will not be compensated for damages that the injured person could have avoided by reasonable effort or expenditure.'" (Mize-Kurzman v. Marin Community College Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 832, 870.) In this case, the evidence demonstrates that Plaintiff mitigated its contractual damages by selling the MIPA projects to other entities and recovering cash and stock in return. (See Talesun's Ex. J [Howell Depo., 114:2-117:24; 120:20-121:8; 122:11-24; 123:13-125:12]; Ex. K [Isern Depo., 34:17-36:24; 41:1-42:12; 48:3-21; 90:6-91:13; 93:13-25; 116:10-16]; Ex. L [Cheney Depo., 73:1-9; 111:16-112:5; 126:2-127:4].) If Plaintiff can establish that the Standstill Agreement is enforceable and Talesun breached it, then Talesun would be entitled to an offset for these payments to prevent double recovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHEW KAM KUNG VS. JINCONG ZHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CHEW KUNG's DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Chew Kam Kung's peremptory challenge of Judge Schulman, filed July 23, 2019, is denied. The challenge is untimely. It was known to Plaintiff at least 10 days before the date set for this hearing that it was assigned to Judge Schulman for hearing, but Plaintiff did not file the challenge at least 5 days before the date of the hearing. The challenge therefore is untimely. (Code Civ. Proc. § 170.6(a)(2).) To the extent that Plaintiff's peremptory challenge was directed to the trial of the remaining claim in this case, which is currently set for January 27, 2020, Judge Schulman, who presides over the law and motion department of this Court, will not be the assigned trial judge. Plaintiff Chew Kam Kung's demurrer to Defendant Jincong Zhen's answer to the third amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiff has not presented any legal argument or judicially noticeable facts warranting relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHEW KAM KUNG VS. JINCONG ZHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate From Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CHEW KUNG's Motion To Vacate From Order. Plaintiff Chew Kam Kung's peremptory challenge of Judge Schulman, filed July 23, 2019, is denied. This motion seeks to vacate a May 9, 2019 order by Judge Schulman. Because Plaintiff's motion to vacate that order is a continuation of the prior proceeding, the peremptory challenge is stricken. (See Jacobs v. Superior Court (1959) 53 Cal.2d 187, 192 [a peremptory challenge "cannot be entertained as to subsequent hearings which are a part or a continuation of the original proceedings"]; Pickett v. Superior Court (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 887, 893 [rule applies in "a subsequent proceeding, the gravamen of which is rooted in, or supplementary to, the initial proceeding"].) Plaintiff Chew Kam Kung's motion to vacate the Court's May 9, 2019 order overruling in part and sustaining in part defendant's demurrer to third amended complaint is denied. Although Plaintiff asserts that the basis for the motion is excusable mistake, he has not made any factual showing or presented any legal argument warranting relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FLEXIBLE FUNDING LTD, LIABILITY CO. VS. TIJUANA'S PRODUCE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 24, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT TIJUANA'S PRODUCE, INC. MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Defendant Tijuana's Produce, Inc.'s motion to change venue is denied. On a motion to change venue, the burden is on the moving party to show (1) that venue is proper in a different county than the one in which the lawsuit was filed and (2) that venue is improper in the current county under any applicable theory. (La Mirada Community Hospital v. Superior Court (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 39, 42.) Although Defendant has established that Los Angeles County would be a proper venue, it has failed to show that venue is improper in San Francisco County under any applicable theory. The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
